---
title: "Ethers.js vs the Aptos TS SDK"
description: "Map familiar Ethers-style flows to the current Aptos TypeScript SDK, Wallet Adapter, and Forklift workflow."
sidebar:
  label: "Ethers.js vs TS SDK"
---

Ethereum frontend developers usually start with Ethers.js, a provider, a signer, and a contract instance. On Aptos, the equivalent stack is:

- [`@aptos-labs/ts-sdk`](/build/sdks/ts-sdk) for reading data, building transactions, and submitting them
- [Wallet Adapter](/build/sdks/wallet-adapter) for browser wallet integration using the current AIP-62 standard
- [Forklift](/build/sdks/forklift) for a Hardhat/Foundry-like contract development workflow

## Ethers-style mental model

```typescript
const provider = new ethers.JsonRpcProvider(rpcUrl);
const wallet = new ethers.Wallet(privateKey, provider);

const tx = await wallet.sendTransaction({
  to: recipient,
  value: ethers.parseEther("1"),
});

await tx.wait();
```

## Current Aptos SDK flow

```typescript
import {
  Account,
  Aptos,
  AptosConfig,
  Network,
} from "@aptos-labs/ts-sdk";

const aptos = new Aptos(new AptosConfig({ network: Network.TESTNET }));
const alice = Account.generate();
const bob = Account.generate();

await aptos.fundAccount({
  accountAddress: alice.accountAddress,
  amount: 100_000_000,
});

const transaction = await aptos.transaction.build.simple({
  sender: alice.accountAddress,
  data: {
    function: "0x1::aptos_account::transfer",
    functionArguments: [bob.accountAddress, 1_000],
  },
});

const pending = await aptos.signAndSubmitTransaction({
  signer: alice,
  transaction,
});

await aptos.waitForTransaction({ transactionHash: pending.hash });
```

## What to update in your instincts

- Build the transaction first, then sign and submit it.
- Always call `waitForTransaction()` after submission.
- Use the [Wallet Adapter](/build/sdks/wallet-adapter) for browser wallets instead of old `window.aptos` style globals.
- Use [Forklift](/build/sdks/forklift) when you want a contract-development workflow closer to Hardhat or Foundry.

For more advanced flows, continue with [building transactions](/build/sdks/ts-sdk/building-transactions), [sponsored transactions](/build/guides/sponsored-transactions), and [wallet adapter dapp integration](/build/sdks/wallet-adapter/dapp).
